title: Fabric Empress
description: Live e-commerce website built for a textile business. Deployed and serving real customers.
longDescription: A fully deployed, production-ready e-commerce website built for a textile and fabric retail client. Features a clean product catalog, elegant responsive design, smooth navigation, and is currently live on Vercel serving real users. Built with a focus on visual polish and brand identity — showcasing fabrics, collections, and making product discovery intuitive.

title: TripNest — Trip Planner
description: MVP full-stack travel planning web app built for a client. Core trip management features complete.
longDescription: A full-stack travel planning platform built as an MVP for a client. Handles trip itinerary creation, destination browsing, and basic booking flows with a premium responsive UI. Built with a Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend, and a PostgreSQL database. The MVP covers core trip management — the next phase focuses on payment integration and live API connections.
whatRemains: Payment gateway integration, live hotel and flight APIs, user profile system, mobile app, itinerary PDF export.

title: Data Clean Fast
description: Bilingual EN/UR data profiling and cleaning web app — currently under active development.
longDescription: A high-performance, bilingual (English/Urdu) data cleaning and profiling application aimed at analysts. Features a FastAPI backend for ML-assisted data profiling, column type detection, and transformation operations, paired with a React frontend featuring an interactive data grid with real-time manipulation. The tool is actively being developed with Auto Profiling Dashboard and Transform Builder as current focus areas.
features: Interactive data grid with real-time edits
Bilingual English / Urdu UI with RTL support
whatRemains: Interactive Transform Builder, Issue Flagging system, multi-file support, collaboration mode, and final production deploy.

title: House Price Predictor — King County
description: ML regression model predicting house prices using the King County, WA dataset. EDA and modelling complete.
longDescription: A machine learning project that builds regression models to predict house prices in King County, Washington using the publicly available Kaggle dataset. Applies exploratory data analysis, feature engineering (log transforms, interaction terms), and multiple regression algorithms including Linear Regression, Ridge, Lasso, and Random Forest. The modelling pipeline is partially complete — the next step is a clean interactive dashboard and deployment.
features: Multiple regression models compared (Linear, Ridge, Lasso, RF)
Correlation heatmap and feature importance plots
whatRemains: Bug fixes in the preprocessing pipeline, an interactive Streamlit or Gradio dashboard, and deployment as a live demo.

title: GO Board Game
description: Console-based C++ implementation of the classic GO board game — built as Programming Fundamentals coursework.
longDescription: A console-based implementation of the classic strategy board game GO, written in C++ as part of the Programming Fundamentals course. Implements turn-based gameplay, stone placement, basic capture logic, and territory-based scoring on a configurable board size. This is an early-stage version — the core game logic works, but the presentation is purely text-based and the game flow can be further refined.
features: Move validation and illegal move prevention
whatRemains: Graphical UI (SFML or Qt), improved game flow, full KO rule enforcement, score visualisation, and an optional AI opponent.

title: Stronghold
description: Console-based C++ fortress defense game demonstrating OOP principles — inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation.
longDescription: A console-based medieval fortress defense game developed as an Object-Oriented Programming course project in C++. Demonstrates core OOP concepts through a deeply structured entity hierarchy — soldiers, siege weapons, and buildings all inherit from base classes and interact through well-defined interfaces. Features resource management, wave-based enemy attacks, and a turn-based strategy loop.
features: Full OOP design with deep class hierarchies
Multiple soldier and building unit types
whatRemains: Graphical UI using SFML, more interactive attack animations, map system, multiplayer mode, and a difficulty scaling system.

title: Job Scheduler
description: Console-based C++ simulation of classic CPU scheduling algorithms using custom data structures.
longDescription: A console-based simulation of foundational CPU scheduling algorithms implemented in C++ using custom data structures as part of a Data Structures course. Implements FCFS, SJF (preemptive and non-preemptive), Round Robin, and Priority Scheduling. Outputs per-process waiting time, turnaround time, and average metrics. The logic is solid — the next step is adding a visual Gantt chart and a proper interactive interface.
features: FCFS, SJF, Round Robin, and Priority algorithms
Custom Queue and Linked List implementations
Per-process waiting time and turnaround time
whatRemains: Visual Gantt chart output, interactive process input UI, side-by-side algorithm comparison mode, and a web-based interface.

title: Brick Breaker
description: Classic arcade Brick Breaker game written in x86 Assembly Language, rendered via DOSBox.
longDescription: A low-level Brick Breaker arcade game written entirely in x86 Assembly Language (MASM/TASM) and rendered through DOSBox as part of a Computer Organization and Assembly Language (COAL) course project. Implements direct hardware-level pixel drawing, real-time interrupt-driven keyboard input, collision detection, and a scoring system — all without any high-level library support.
features: Pure Assembly — no libraries or OS APIs
Real-time keyboard input via hardware interrupts
Ball physics and brick collision detection
whatRemains: More interactive controls, additional levels, high-score persistence, sound effects via PC speaker, and a more polished visual theme.
